title: Don't override from_email / hello_name — you bypass the warmed, PTR-matched rotation
summary: The worker rotates across 50 pre-warmed sending identities, each on an IP whose reverse-DNS (PTR) matches its EHLO/HELO hostname. That PTR<->EHLO match is the single most important factor in not getting rejected by Gmail/Outlook — a mismatch causes >50% rejection, which surfaces to the client as inflated unknown/risky rates and WORSE accuracy. A fresh from_email has zero sender reputation (warmup takes 2-4 weeks) and gets greylisted. The payload lets you override both (from_email/hello_name), but empty/omitted = the worker's warmed rotated identity, which is almost always what you want. Only override as a MATCHED pair if you operate your own warmed infra with matching PTR.
references: docs/services/spiderverifyer/LEARNINGS.md:195-204 — PTR record is the most critical DNS config (mismatch >50% rejection)
docs/services/spiderverifyer/LEARNINGS.md:155-160 — warmup requires 2-4 weeks before reliable results
docs/services/spiderverifyer/LEARNINGS.md:106-111 — rotation across warmed identities; static fallback defeats it
workers/SpiderVerify/verification_engine.py:255-281 — _build_reacher_request: payload override OR rotator OR static
